config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan melindungi hak dasar setiap orang, bukan mengorbankannya begitu saja. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because in practice that often becomes an excuse for authority to override ordinary people. From my perspective as someone from China, social order and collective interests are very important, and people do tend to value harmony and responsibility to the group, but that does not mean individual rights should be treated as secondary by default. If the state or society can always claim the greater good, then minorities and weaker individuals can easily be ignored. A fair society needs balance: protect basic rights first, then pursue the common interest within those limits.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis lebih tinggi daripada hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya sebagai orang Indonesia, kita memang sering diajari gotong royong dan kepentingan bersama, tetapi itu tidak boleh dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau mengorbankan hak dasar seseorang. Kepentingan publik penting, namun harus dibatasi oleh perlindungan hak individu yang jelas; kalau tidak, “demi masyarakat” gampang sekali berubah jadi alasan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Jadi, menurut saya yang benar bukan mendahulukan salah satunya secara mutlak, melainkan menjaga keseimbangan dengan hak individu tetap menjadi pagar utamanya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in China, people do value harmony, stability, and collective responsibility, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to silence ordinary people or weaken protections for those with less power. The common interest matters, but it should be pursued within clear limits that protect basic rights first, especially for minorities and individuals who cannot defend themselves. If society always comes first, then the word society can become a convenient excuse for unfairness.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, gotong royong memang penting, tetapi justru nilai itu sehat kalau tidak dipakai untuk menekan orang yang berbeda pendapat, minoritas, atau warga yang sedang lemah posisinya. Argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa, jadi hak dasar individu harus jadi pagar utama. Kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi mestinya dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From my perspective as someone from China, collective goals and social stability are real values, but I have also seen how easily “for the good of society” can become a blank check for power to ignore ordinary people, especially those with less influence. In practice, the rights of the individual should be the baseline, because once that baseline is weakened, minority voices and unpopular people are the first to suffer. A healthy society is not one that asks individuals to disappear, but one that protects their basic rights while still encouraging responsibility to the group.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
